{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Burkina Faso women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Burkina Faso
| Badge              = 
| Nickname           = ''Les Étalons''<br />(The [[Stallion]]s)<br />''Les faucons pèlerins''<br />(The [[Peregrine falcon|Peregrine Falcons]])
| Association        = [[Burkinabé Football Federation|Fédération Burkinabé de Football]] (FBF)
| Sub-confederation  = [[West African Football Union|WAFU]] (West Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach              = [[Amir Abdou]]
| Captain            = [[Bertrand Traoré]]
| Most caps          = [[Charles Kaboré]] (102)
| Top scorer         = {{nowrap|[[Moumouni Dagano]] (34)}}<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/century.html |title=Players with 100+ Caps and 30+ International Goals |author1=Mamrud, Roberto |author2=Stokkermans, Karel |access-date=2011-03-27 |url-status=live |website=[[RSSSF]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110628192800/http://www.rsssf.com/miscellaneous/century.html |archive-date=2011-06-28 }}</ref>
| Home Stadium       = [[Stade du 4-Août]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = BFA
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|BFA}}
| FIFA max           = 35
| FIFA max date      = April–May 2017
| FIFA min           = 127
| FIFA min date      = December 1993
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Burkina Faso}}
| Elo max            = 52
| Elo max date       = September 2017
| Elo min            = 133
| Elo min date       = February 1987
| pattern_la1        = _bfa24h
| pattern_b1         = _bfa24h
| pattern_ra1        = _bfa24h
| pattern_sh1        = _bfa24h
| pattern_so1        = _bfa24h
| leftarm1           = 1c9340
| body1              = 006000
| rightarm1          = 1c9340
| shorts1            = 1c9340
| socks1             = 1c9340
| pattern_la2        = _bfa24a
| pattern_b2         = _bfa24a
| pattern_ra2        = _bfa24a
| pattern_sh2        = _bfa24a
| pattern_so2        = _bfa24a
| leftarm2           = FFFFFF
| body2              = FFFFFF
| rightarm2          = FFFFFF
| shorts2            = FFFFFF
| socks2             = FFFFFF
| pattern_la3        = _bfa24t
| pattern_b3         = _bfa24t
| pattern_ra3        = _bfa24t
| pattern_sh3        = _bfa24t
| pattern_so3        = _bfa24t
| leftarm3           = fff200
| body3              = fff200
| rightarm3          = fff200
| shorts3            = fff200
| socks3             = fff200
| First game         = {{fb|Upper Volta}} 5–4 {{fb-rt|GAB|1959}}<br />([[Antananarivo|Tananarive]], [[Madagascar]]; 14 April 1960)
| Largest win        = {{fb|DJI}} 0–6 {{fb-rt|Burkina Faso}}<br />([[Bissau]], [[Guinea-Bissau]]; 5 September 2025)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|ALG}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|Upper Volta}}<br />([[Oran]], [[Algeria]]; 30 August 1981)
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 14
| Regional cup first = [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]]
| Regional cup best  = Runners-up ([[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]])
|2ndRegional cup apps=
|2ndRegional name=
|2ndRegional cup first=
|2ndRegional cup best=
}}

The '''Burkina Faso national football team''' ({{langx|fr|Équipe de football du Burkina Faso}}) represents [[Burkina Faso]] in men's international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Burkinabé Football Federation]]. They were known as the '''Upper Volta national football team''' until 1984, when [[Republic of Upper Volta|Upper Volta]] became Burkina Faso. They finished fourth in the [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations]], when they hosted the tournament. Their best ever finish in the tournament was the [[2013 African Cup of Nations|2013 edition]], reaching the [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations final|final]].

==History==
Their first international match was played on April 13, 1960, in the [[Jeux de la Communauté]] in [[Madagascar]] and ended with a 5–4 victory against [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]].

===Africa Cup of Nations===
The country made their first appearance in the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] in [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]], but it was not until [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]] that they returned to the biennial tournament. They subsequently qualified for five consecutive tournaments between 1996 and 2004, reaching the semi-finals under coach [[Philippe Troussier]] when the tournament was held on home soil in 1998.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/3367555.stm|title=Burkina Faso fancy their chances|work=BBC Sport|date=16 January 2004|access-date=17 January 2013|archive-date=6 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160306162359/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/3367555.stm|url-status=live}}</ref>

Burkina Faso played in Group B of the [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations]] alongside Ghana and Ivory Coast in a three-team group due to Togo's withdrawal. Although they drew their first match against Ivory Coast and needed just a draw against Ghana to progress, the Burkinabe lost 1–0 and failed to qualify for the knock-out stage of the tournament.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8466211.stm|title=Burkina Faso 0–1 Ghana|work=BBC Sport|date=19 January 2010|access-date=17 January 2013|archive-date=1 March 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170301163621/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8466211.stm|url-status=live}}</ref> Burkina Faso took part in the [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations]], losing all three of their matches and subsequently firing coach [[Paulo Jorge Rebelo Duarte|Paulo Duarte]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/17075218|title=Burkina Faso coach gets the boot|work=BBC Sport|date=17 February 2012|access-date=17 January 2013|archive-date=22 February 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120222043153/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/football/17075218|url-status=live}}</ref> Belgian coach [[Paul Put]] was announced as new coach in March 2012.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/17499129|title=Former Gambia coach Put handed reins at Burkina Faso|work=BBC Sport|date=24 March 2012|access-date=17 January 2013|archive-date=28 March 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120328025416/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/football/17499129|url-status=live}}</ref>
Burkina Faso finished first of their group, but lost to Nigeria in the final of the [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations]].<ref>{{cite web|url= http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/blogs/world-of-sport/mba-wondergoal-wins-african-cup-nations-nigeria-090415139.html|title= Mba's wondergoal wins African Cup of Nations for Nigeria|date= 10 February 2013|work= Eurosport|access-date= 12 February 2013|archive-date= 15 February 2013|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20130215020209/http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/blogs/world-of-sport/mba-wondergoal-wins-african-cup-nations-nigeria-090415139.html|url-status= live}}</ref>

The team would earn third place at the [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations]].

===World Cup qualifying===
Burkina Faso first entered the World Cup in the [[1978 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1978 qualifying tournament]], beating [[Mauritania national football team|Mauritania]] in the preliminary round before losing 1–3 against [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]]. They next entered World Cup qualifying in [[1990 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1990]], losing in the first round to [[Libya national football team|Libya]] 2–3. Burkina Faso withdrew from the 1994 competition but returned in [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1998]], beating [[Mauritania national football team|Mauritania]] again to make it to the final qualifying group stage, however they failed to obtain a single point, finishing bottom of their group. They beat [[Ethiopia national football team|Ethiopia]] in [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2002]] to again make it to the qualifying group stage, but did not advance, only winning one game against [[Malawi national football team|Malawi]].

The team had a strong showing in the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2014 World Cup qualification]] campaign, reaching the final round of qualifying where it faced [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]]. It won 3–2 in Ouagadougou, but lost 1–0 in [[Blida]]. Despite the 3-3 aggregate, Burkina Faso narrowly missed out on the [[2014 World Cup]] due to the [[away goals rule]].

==Nickname==
The team is nicknamed ''Les Etalons'', which means "The Stallions".  It is in reference to the legendary horse of Princess [[Yennenga]].<ref>{{cite book | last = Marchais | first = Julien | title = Burkina Faso | publisher = Petit Futé | page = 102 | url = https://books.google.com/books?id=6jsBLSzJWYsC | language = fr | isbn = 2-7469-1601-0 | year = 2006 | access-date = 2016-09-23 | archive-date = 2023-04-09 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230409083621/https://books.google.com/books?id=6jsBLSzJWYsC | url-status = live }}</ref> Supporters of the team at times include a percussion band, which often mimics the sounds of galloping horses at matches.

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Burkina Faso national football team results (2020–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|id=Tunisia V Burkina Faso|date = 2 June
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|time       = {{UTZ|18:30|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|TUN}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =[[Edmond Tapsoba|Tapsoba]] {{goal|60|o.g.}}<br />[[Hazem Mastouri|Hazem]] {{goal|90+1}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Hammadi Agrebi Stadium]]
|location   = [[Tunis]], Tunisia
|attendance = 
|referee    = Lahlou Benbraham ([[Algeria Football Federation|Algeria]])
|result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 5 September
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|DJI}}
| score      = 0–6
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
| goals1     =[[Yabe Siad Isman|Siad Isman]] {{sent off|0|10}} 
| goals2     =[[Cyriaque Irié|Irié]] {{goal|16}}<br />[[Wendkuuni Josué Tiendrébéogo|Tiendrébéogo]] {{goal|25}}<br />[[Edmond Tapsoba|Tapsoba]] {{goal|36||42}}<br />[[Dango Ouattara|Ouattara]] {{goal|59||86|pen.}}
| stadium    = [[Estádio 24 de Setembro]]
| location   = [[Bissau]], Guinea-Bissau
| attendance = 
| referee    = Tawel Camara ([[Guinean Football Federation|Guinea]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 9 September
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 0–0
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|EGY}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], Burkina Faso
| attendance = 
| referee    = Messie Nkounkou ([[Congolese Football Federation|Congo]])
| result     = D
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 8 October
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SLE}}
| score      = 0–1
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = [[Mohamed Zougrana|Zougrana]] {{goal|42}}
| stadium    = [[Samuel Kanyon Doe Sports Complex]]
| location   = [[Paynesville, Liberia|Paynesville]], Liberia
| attendance = 
| referee    = [[Jean-Jacques Ndala]] ([[Congolese Association Football Federation|DR Congo]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 12 October
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 3–1 
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|ETH}}
| goals1     = [[Pierre Landry Kabore|Kabore]] {{goal|65||82||90+6}}
| goals2     = [[Biniam Ayten|Ayten]] {{goal|84}}
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], Burkina Faso
| attendance = 
| referee    = Adalbert Diouf ([[Senegalese Football Federation|Senegal]])
| result     = W 
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 14 November
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 3–2
| report     = https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/760878/niger-burkina-faso
| team2      = {{fb|NIG}}
| goals1     = [[Pierre Landry Kabore|Kabore]] {{goal|34||45+3||57}}
| goals2     = [[Ousseini Badamassi|Badamassi]] {{goal|24}}<br />[[Daniel Sosah|Sosah]] {{goal|55}}
| stadium    = [[El Bachir Stadium]]
| location   = [[Mohammedia]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 18 November
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 3–0
| report     = https://www.panafricafootball.com/post/burkina-faso-4/
| team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Georgi Minoungou|Minoungou]] {{goal|25||45+3}}
*[[Saidou Simpore|Simpore]] {{goal|90+4}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[El Bachir Stadium]]
| location   = [[Mohammedia]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|date       = 24 December
|time       = {{UTZ|13:30|1}}
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations Group E|2025 AFCON GS]]
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
|score      = 2–1
|team2      = {{fb|EQG}}
|goals1     = *[[Georgi Minoungou|Minoungou]] {{goal|90+5}}
*[[Edmond Tapsoba|Tapsoba]] {{goal|90+8}}
|goals2     = *[[Basilio Ndong|Ndong]] {{sentoff|0|50}}
*[[Marvin Anieboh|Anieboh]] {{goal|85}}
|stadium    = [[Mohammed V Stadium]]
|location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance = 10,267
|referee    = Mohamed Maarouf ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/400132623/1z35p4iuhfxxdfaqjwzkqn2fo/207mir3y73ql1144i13i8xe6s/dxgabsuyghs10cap88adaq13o?date=2025-12-24
|result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|date       = 28 December
|time       = {{UTZ|18:30|1}}
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations Group E|2025 AFCON GS]]
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ALG}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|BUR}}
|goals1     = [[Riyad Mahrez|Mahrez]] {{goal|23|pen}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Moulay Hassan Stadium]]
|location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance = 18,522
|referee    = [[Daniel Nii Laryea]] ([[Ghana Football Association|Ghana]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506794
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|date       = 31 December
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations Group E|2025 AFCON GS]]
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SDN}}
|score      = 0–2
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     = *[[Lassina Traoré|L. Traoré]] {{goal|16}}
*[[Arsène Kouassi|Kouassi]] {{goal|85}}
|stadium    = [[Mohammed V Stadium]]
|location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance = 10,084
|referee    = [[Amin Mohamed Omar]] ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506807
|result     = W
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations knockout stage|2025 AFCON R16]]
|date       = 6 January
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CIV}}
|score      = 3–0
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|goals1     = 
*[[Amad Diallo|Amad]] {{goal|20}}
*[[Yan Diomande|Y. Diomande]] {{goal|32}}
*[[Bazoumana Touré|Touré]] {{goal|87}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Marrakesh Stadium]]
|location   = [[Marrakesh]], Morocco
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Mahmood Ismail]] ([[Sudan Football Association|Sudan]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506817
|result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 28 March
| time       = {{UTZ|19:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 5–0
| report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-28/burkina-faso-vs-guinea-bissau/4137494
| team2      = {{fb|GNB}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Pierre Landry Kaboré|P. Kaboré]] {{goal|26}}
*[[Dango Ouattara|Ouattara]] {{goal|40}}
*[[Elohim Kaboré|E. Kaboré]] {{goal|61||73||88}}
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], Burkina Faso
| attendance = 
| referee    = Moussa Ahamadou Alou ([[Nigerien Football Federation|Niger]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 31 March
| time       = {{UTZ|18:00|0}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 1–1
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|GNB}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Adamo Nagalo|Nagalo]] {{goal|85}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Fuscano Gomes|F. Gomes]] {{goal|90+6}}
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], Burkina Faso
| attendance = 
| referee    = Moussa Ahmadou Alou ([[Nigerien Football Federation|Niger]])
| result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date       = 5 June
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|+3}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|RUS}}
|score      = 3–0
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|report     = https://www.soccerway.com/match/burkina-faso-bJOXzWga/russia-hrgrswHh/?mid=fJz9NRBE
|goals1     =
*[[Lechi Sadulayev|Sadulayev]] {{goal|15}}
*[[Aleksei Miranchuk|Al. Miranchuk]] {{goal|20}}
*[[Ilya Vakhaniya|Vakhaniya]] {{goal|73}}
|goals2     =
|location   = [[Volgograd]], Russia
|stadium    = [[Volgograd Arena]]
|attendance = 41,035
|referee    = Nenad Minaković ([[Football Association of Serbia|Serbia]])
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|date = 9 June
|time = {{UTZ|19:30|3}}
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|team1 = {{fb-rt|BLR}}
|score = 2–2
|report = https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/friendlies/match/2048315--belarus-vs-burkina-faso/
|team2 = {{fb|BFA}}
|goals1 =
*[[Vladislav Malkevich (footballer)|Malkevich]] {{goal|56}}
*[[Karen Vardanyan (footballer)|Vardanyan]] {{goal|67}}
|goals2 =
*[[Elohim Kaboré|Kaboré]] {{goal|72}}
*[[Abdoul Tapsoba|Tapsoba]] {{goal|85}}
|location = [[Minsk]], Belarus 
|stadium = [[National Football Stadium (Belarus)|National Football Stadium]]
|attendance = 15,874
|referee = Rafael Shafeev ([[Russian Football Federation|Russia]])
|result = D
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BEN}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Burkina Faso
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Benin
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|MTN}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Burkina Faso
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MTN}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Mauritania
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|CTA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Burkina Faso
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CTA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|BFA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Central African Republic
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
!Position
!Name
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|COM}} [[Amir Abdou]]
|-
|Assistant coaches
|{{flagicon|ALG}} Bouziane Benaraïbi<br />{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Jonathan Zongo]]
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|BFA}} Aurélien Yaméogo
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|FRA}} Julien Redon
|-
|Match analyst
|{{flagicon|BFA}} Ismaïla Boussouma
|-
|Doctor
|{{flagicon|BFA}} Dr. Cheick Tiendrebéogo
|-
|Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|BFA}} Moussa Kiemdé<br />{{flagicon|BFA}} Christophe Zongo<br />{{flagicon|BFA}} Boubacarr Tapsoba<br />{{flagicon|BFA}} Évariste Nikiéma
|-
|Team coordinator
|{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Charles Kaboré]]
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Kamou Malo]]
|}

===Coaching history===
[[File:Gernot Rohr 2018.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Gernot Rohr]] became the manager of Burkina Faso in 2015]]

{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
*{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Otto Pfister]] (1976–1978)
*{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Heinz-Peter Überjahn]] (1988–1990)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Idrissa Traoré (footballer, born 1943)|Idrissa Traoré]] (1992–1996)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Calixte Zagre]] (1996)
*{{flagicon|BUL}} [[Ivan Vutov]] (1996–1997)
*{{flagicon|GHA}} [[Malik Jabir]] (1997)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Philippe Troussier]] (1997–1998)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Didier Notheaux]] (1998–1999)
*{{flagicon|BEL}} [[René Taelman]] (2000)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Sidiki Diarra]] (2000–2001)
*{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Oscar Fulloné]] (2001–2002)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Jacques Yaméogo]] & {{flagicon|BFA}} [[Pihouri Weboanga]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Jean-Paul Rabier]] (2002–2004)
*{{flagicon|SCG}} [[Ivica Todorov]] (2004–2005)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Bernard Simondi]] (2005–2006)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Idrissa Traoré (footballer, born 1943)|Idrissa Traoré]] (2006–2007)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Didier Notheaux]] & {{flagicon|BFA}} [[Sidiki Diarra]] (2007)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Paulo Jorge Rebelo Duarte]] (2008–2012)
*{{flagicon|BEL}} [[Paul Put]] (2012–2015)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Gernot Rohr]] (2015)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Paulo Duarte (footballer)|Paulo Duarte]] (2015–2019)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Kamou Malo]] (2019–2022)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Hubert Velud]] (2022–2024)
*{{flagicon|BFA}} [[Brama Traoré]] (2024–2026)
*{{flagicon|COM}} [[Amir Abdou]] (2026–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] matches against [[Russia national football team|Russia]] and [[Belarus national football team|Belarus]] on 5 and 9 June 2026; respectively.<ref>{{cite news |title=Journées FIFA du Mois de Juin |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1001490712414615&set=a.207592508471110&locale=en_GB |access-date=27 May 2026 |work=Facebook |publisher=Fédération Burkinabè de Football |date=19 May 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 9 June 2026, after the match against [[Belarus national football team|Belarus]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Farid Ouédraogo]]|age={{bda|1996|12|26|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Al Hilal Club (Omdurman)|Al Hilal]]|clubnat=SUD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=GK|name=[[Hillel Konaté]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|12|28|df=yes}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[ES Troyes AC|Troyes]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[Kilian Nikiema]]|age={{bda|2003|6|22|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[ADO Den Haag]]|clubnat=NED}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Hervé Koffi]]|age={{bda|1996|10|16|df=y}}|caps=70|goals=0|club=[[Angers SCO|Angers]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Mohamed Ouédraogo]]|age={{bda|2003|1|2|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[SC Rheindorf Altach|Rheindorf Altach]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Adamo Nagalo]]|age={{bda|2002|9|22|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=[[Konyaspor]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=Cyril Dao|age={{birth date and age|2005|11|10|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[National Bank of Egypt SC|National Bank of Egypt]]|clubnat=EGY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=DF|name=[[Edmond Tapsoba]]|age={{bda|1999|2|2|df=y}}|caps=62|goals=5|club=[[Bayer 04 Leverkusen|Bayer Leverkusen]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Rachide Gnanou]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|11|16|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[IF Gnistan|Gnistan]]|clubnat=FIN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=DF|name=[[Arthur Zagré]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|10|04|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Excelsior Rotterdam|Excelsior]]|clubnat=NED}}
{{nat fs g player|no=24|pos=DF|name=[[Saïdou Simporé]]|age={{bda|1992|8|31|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=1|club=[[National Bank of Egypt SC|National Bank of Egypt]]|clubnat=EGY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=25|pos=DF|name=Hanaby Hadalou Sagne|age={{birth date and age|2000|12|20|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS Douanes (Burkina Faso)|AS Douanes]]|clubnat=BFA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=26|pos=DF|name=[[Arsène Kouassi]]|age={{bda|2004|9|11|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=1|club=[[FC Lorient]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nasser Djiga]]|age={{bda|2002|11|15|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=1|club=[[Rangers F.C.|Rangers]]|clubnat=SCO}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=MF|name=[[Aboubacar Bassinga]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|07|13|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AD Ceuta|Ceuta]]|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Mohamed Zougrana]]|age={{bda|2001|10|29|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=2|club=[[MC Alger]]|clubnat=ALG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Lohann Doucet]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|09|14|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[USL Dunkerque|Dunkerque]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=MF|name=[[Ismahila Ouédraogo]]|age={{bda|1999|11|5|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=0|club=[[Odense Boldklub|Odense]]|clubnat=DEN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Gustavo Sangaré]]|age={{bda|1996|11|8|df=y}}|caps=40|goals=2|club=[[FC Noah|Noah]]|clubnat=ARM}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[Dango Ouattara]]|age={{bda|2002|2|11|df=y}}|caps=40|goals=12|club=[[Brentford F.C.|Brentford]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Ousseni Bouda]]|age={{bda|2000|4|28|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=2|club=[[San Jose Earthquakes]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=FW|name=[[Mohamed Konaté (footballer, born 1997)|Mohamed Konaté]]|age={{bda|1997|12|12|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=6|club=[[FC Akhmat Grozny|Akhmat Grozny]]|clubnat=RUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=FW|name=[[Abdoul Tapsoba]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|08|23|df=yes}}|caps=30|goals=5|club=[[Radomiak Radom]]|clubnat=POL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=[[Georgi Minoungou]]|age={{bda|2002|7|25|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Colorado Rapids]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=FW|name=[[Pierre Landry Kaboré]]|age={{bda|2001|7|5|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=3|club=[[Heart of Midlothian F.C.|Heart of Midlothian]]|clubnat=SCO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=FW|name=[[Cyriaque Irié]]|age={{bda|2005|6|20|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=2|club=[[SC Freiburg]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=29|pos=FW|name=[[Elohim Kaboré]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|12|20|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[Hammarby Fotboll|Hammarby]]|clubnat=SWE}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up for Burkina Faso in the last twelve months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=Ladji Brahima Sanou|age={{bda|2003|4|21|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Al-Merrikh SC|Al-Merrikh]]|clubnat=SUD|latest=v. {{fb|ETH}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Issa Kaboré]]|age={{bda|2001|5|12|df=y}}|caps=52|goals=2|club=[[Wrexham A.F.C|Wrexham]]|clubnat=WAL|latest=v. {{fb|GNB}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Dylan Ouédraogo]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|07|22|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Krasava ENY Ypsonas FC|Krasava ENY]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|GNB}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=25|pos=DF|name=[[Steeve Yago]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|third captain]]|age={{bda|1992|12|16|df=y}}|caps=93|goals=1|club=[[Aris Limassol FC|Aris Limassol]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Issoufou Dayo]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|vice-captain]]|age={{bda|1991|8|6|df=y}}|caps=84|goals=9|club=[[Umm Salal SC|Umm-Salal]]|clubnat=QAT|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Abdoul Ayinde]]|age={{bda|2005|7|17|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[K.A.A. Gent|Gent]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ismaëlo Ganiou]]|age={{bda|2005|3|14|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[RC Lens|Lens]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=v. {{fb|ZIM}}, 6 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Hassane Rachid Traoré|age={{bda|2007|7|13|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[US des Forces Armées|USFA]]|clubnat=BFA|latest=v. {{fb|ZIM}}, 6 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Blati Touré]]|age={{bda|1994|8|4|df=y}}|caps=60|goals=3|club=[[Pyramids FC|Pyramids]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|GNB}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ibrahim Bancé]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|01|15|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Marumo Gallants F.C.|Marumo Gallants]]|clubnat=RSA|latest=v. {{fb|GNB}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Cedric Badolo]]|age={{bda|1998|11|4|df=y}}|caps=31|goals=0|club=[[FC Spartak Trnava|Spartak Trnava]]|clubnat=SVK|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=17|pos=MF|name=[[Stephane Aziz Ki]]|age={{bda|1996|3|6|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=3|club=[[Wydad AC|Wydad Casablanca]]|clubnat=MAR|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=28|pos=MF|name=[[Josué Tiendrébéogo]]|age={{bda|2002|11|21|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=2|club=[[FC Annecy|Annecy]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=15|pos=MF|name=[[Abdoul Yoda (footballer, born 2000)|Abdoul Yoda]]|age={{bda|2000|12|20|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[FC Milsami Orhei|Milsami Orhei]]|clubnat=MDA|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Raouf Memel Dao|age={{bda|2003|9|5|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[APR F.C.|APR]]|clubnat=BDI|latest=v. {{fb|ZIM}}, 6 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Lassina Traoré]]|age={{bda|2001|1|12|df=y}}|caps=31|goals=13|club=[[FC Shakhtar Donetsk|Shakhtar Donetsk]]|clubnat=UKR|latest=v. {{fb|GNB}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Jack Diarra|age={{bda|2006|6|16|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Espérance Sportive de Tunis|Espérance de Tunis]]|clubnat=TUN|latest=v. {{fb|GNB}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=10|pos=FW|name=[[Bertrand Traoré]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{bda|1995|9|6|df=y}}|caps=88|goals=21|club=[[Free agent|''Unattached'']]|clubnat=|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Fayçal Konaté|age={{bda|2006|8|15|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FK RFS|RFS]]|clubnat=LVA|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Hassane Bandé]]|age={{bda|1998|10|30|df=y}}|caps=36|goals=3|club=[[K.V. Mechelen|Mechelen]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|ZIM}}, 6 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad.
*<sup>RET</sup> = Player has retired from international football.
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Updated|6 January 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Burkina Faso |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/32/Burkina_Faso.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Burkina Faso.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Charles Kaboré 2019 Dynamo.jpg|thumb|150px|[[Charles Kaboré]] has the most appearances for Burkina Faso with 102.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Charles Kaboré]] || 102 || 4 || 2006–2021
|-
| 2 ||align=left| '''[[Steeve Yago]]''' || 96 || 1 || 2013–present
|-
| 3 ||align=left| '''[[Issoufou Dayo]]''' || 92 || 9 || 2012–present
|-
| 4 ||align=left| '''[[Bertrand Traoré]]''' || 91 || 22 || 2011–present
|-
| 5 ||align=left| [[Jonathan Pitroipa]] || 84 || 19 || 2006–2019
|-
| rowspan=2|6 ||align=left| [[Moumouni Dagano]] || 83 || 34 || 1998–2013
|-
|align=left| [[Bakary Koné]] || 83 || 0 || 2006–2019
|-
| 8 ||align=left| [[Aristide Bancé]] || 79 || 24 || 2003–2019
|-
| 9 ||align=left|  '''[[Hervé Koffi]]''' || 72 || 0 || 2016–present
|-
| 10 ||align=left| [[Saïdou Panandétiguiri]] || 66 || 2 || 2002–2013
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Moumouni Dagano 2011.jpg|thumb|150px|[[Moumouni Dagano]] has the most goals for Burkina Faso with 34.]]
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Moumouni Dagano]] || 34 || 83 || {{#expr:34/83 round 2}} || 1998–2013
|-
| 2 ||align=left| [[Aristide Bancé]] || 24 || 79 || {{#expr:24/79 round 2}} || 2003–2019
|-
| 3 || align=left| '''[[Bertrand Traoré]]''' || 22 || 91 || {{#expr:22/91 round 2}} || 2011–present
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Alain Traoré]] || 21 || 65 || {{#expr:21/65 round 2}} || 2006–2021
|-
| 5 || align=left| [[Jonathan Pitroipa]] || 19 || 84 || {{#expr:19/84 round 2}} || 2006–2019
|-
| 6 ||align=left| '''[[Lassina Traoré]]''' || 15 || 34 || {{#expr:15/34 round 2}} || 2017–present
|-
| rowspan=2|7 ||align=left| [[Mamadou Zongo]] || 13 || 30 || {{#expr:13/30 round 2}} || 1996–2013
|-
|align=left| [[Préjuce Nakoulma]] || 13 || 53 || {{#expr:13/53 round 2}} || 2012–2019
|-
| 9 ||align=left| '''[[Dango Ouattara]]''' || 12 || 40 || {{#expr:12/40 round 2}} || 2021–present
|-
|rowspan=2|10 ||align=left| [[Amadou Touré]] || 10 || 30 || {{#expr:10/30 round 2}} || 1998–2006
|-
|align=left| [[Oumar Barro]] || 10 || 48 || {{#expr:10/48 round 2}} || 1996–2003
|}

==Competitive record==
=== FIFA World Cup ===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=3|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|FRA}}''
|colspan=6|''Part of {{fb|FRA}}''
|-
!colspan=16|as '''{{fb|Upper Volta}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
!rowspan=4|
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4
|1
|2
|1
|4
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
!colspan=16|as '''{{fb|Burkina Faso}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
!rowspan=36|
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=8|'' Did not qualify''
|8
|1
|1
|6
|9
|17
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]] 
|8
|2
|2
|4
|11
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|10||4||1||5||14||13
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|12||9||1||2||24||16
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|8||5||0||3||10||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|8
|3
|3
|2
|13
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|6||3||3||0||12||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10
|6
|3
|1
|23
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total||||{{Tooltip|0/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}||||||||||||
!76
!35
!16
!25
!122
!90
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{football squad on pitch|align=right
| GK = '''[[Abdoulaye Soulama|Soulama]]'''
| LB = '''[[Florent Rouamba|Rouamba]]'''
| LCB = '''[[Bakary Koné|Koné]]'''
| RCB = '''[[Paul Koulibaly|Kéba]]'''
| RB = '''[[Saïdou Panandétiguiri|Panandétiguiri]]'''
| DM = '''[[Charles Kaboré|Kaboré]]'''
| RCM = '''[[Alain Traoré|Traoré]]'''
| LCM = '''[[Djakaridja Koné|Djakaridja]]'''
| LW = '''[[Jonathan Pitroipa|Pitroïpa]]'''
| RW = '''[[Aristide Bancé|Bancé]]'''
| CF = '''[[Moumouni Dagano|Dagano (c)]]'''
| caption = [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations]] starting lineup
}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
!rowspan=41|
!colspan=9|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}} 
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}} 
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}} 
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}} 
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}} 
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}} 
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|France}}'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 African Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
!colspan=9|Played as '''{{fb|Upper Volta}}'''
!colspan=9|Player as '''{{fb|Upper Volta}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4
|0
|0
|4
|2
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Withdrew''
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||0||0||2||1||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]||Group stage||8th||3||0||0||3||2||9||2||0||0||2||1||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 Africa Cup of Nations|1980]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 Africa Cup of Nations|1982]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||0||1||1||1||8
|-
!colspan=9|Played as '''{{fb|Burkina Faso}}'''
!colspan=9|Played as '''{{fb|Burkina Faso}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 Africa Cup of Nations|1984]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 Africa Cup of Nations|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 Africa Cup of Nations|1990]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2||1||0||1||1||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations|1992]]
|8||4||1||3||10||13
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]]||Group stage||15th||3||0||0||3||3||9||4||1||3||0||5||4
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations|1998]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''6'''
|'''2'''
|'''2'''
|'''2'''
|'''8'''
|'''9'''
|colspan=9|''Qualified as hosts''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]
|rowspan=3|Group stage
|15th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|4
|8
|4
|2
|2
|0
|8
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
||13th
||3
||0
||1
||2
||2
||4
||8
||4
||3
||1
||7
||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]
||14th
||3
||0
||1
||2
||1
||6
||6
||4
||2
||0
||12
||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|10||4||1||5||14||13
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|6||1||1||4||5||12
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|rowspan=2|Group stage
|13th
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|1
|12
|9
|1
|2
|24
|16
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|15th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|6
|4
|3
|1
|0
|12
|3
|- style="background:silver;"
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]||'''[[2013 Africa Cup of Nations Final|Runners-up]] '''||'''2nd'''||'''6'''||'''2'''||'''3'''||'''1'''||'''7'''||'''3'''||2||1||0||1||2||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]||Group stage||16th||3||0||1||2||1||4||6||3||2||1||8||4
|-bgcolor="#cc9966"
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]||'''Third place'''||'''3rd'''||'''6'''||'''3'''||'''3'''||'''0'''||'''8'''||'''3'''||6||4||1||1||6||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]||style="text-align:center;" colspan=8|''Did not qualify''||6||3||1||2||8||5
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]||'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''7'''||'''2'''||'''3'''||'''2'''||'''9'''||'''10'''||6||3||3||0||6||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]||rowspan=2|Round of 16
|13th
|4
|1
|1
|2
|4
|6
|6
|3
|2
|1
|8
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|11th
|4
|2
|0
|2
|4
|5
||6||3||1||2||10||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8"|''To be determined''
| colspan="6"|''To be determined''
|-
!Total
!Runners-up
!14/35
!56
!12
!17
!27
!55
!83
!112
!53
!26
!33
!151
!133
|}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 3
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|SUD}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]|| |Group stage||13th||3||0||1||2||2||4
|-
|{{flagicon|RWA}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]||colspan=8 |''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]|| |Group stage||11th||3||0||2||1||1||3
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]|| |Group stage||9th||3||1||1||1||3||2
|-
|{{flagicon|ALG}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]||colspan=8 |''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|KEN}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]||colspan=8 |''To be determined''
|-
!Total||Group stage ||3/8||9||1||4||4||6||9
|}

===African Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the African Games|African Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Pld
!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[Football at the 1973 All-Africa Games|1973]]||8th||3||0||0||3||4||10
|-
!Total||1/4|||||||||||||
|}

===West African Nations Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[West African Nations Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Benin|1975}} [[1982 West African Nations Cup|1982]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1983 West African Nations Cup|1983]]
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1984 West African Nations Cup|1984]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''5'''
|'''1'''
|'''3'''
|'''1'''
|'''6'''
|'''7'''
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1986 West African Nations Cup|1986]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''6'''
|'''1'''
|'''1'''
|'''4'''
|'''2'''
|'''7'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Liberia}} [[1987 West African Nations Cup|1987]]
|Group stage
|5th
|3
|1
|0
|2
|3
|4
|-
!Total
!Fourth place
!3/5
!14
!3
!4
!7
!11
!18
|}

===WAFU Nations Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[WAFU Nations Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- style="background:#9acdff;"
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2010 WAFU Nations Cup|2010]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''5'''
|'''2'''
|'''0'''
|'''3'''
|'''9'''
|'''8'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2011 WAFU Nations Cup|2011]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2013 WAFU Nations Cup|2013]]
|Group stage
|5th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|3
|5
|-
!Total
!Fourth place
!2/3
!8
!2
!1
!5
!12
!13
|}

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[Africa Cup of Nations]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]

===Regional===
* '''[[UEMOA Tournament]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1):''' 2013
* '''[[West African Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): 1982

===Friendly===
* '''[[Mapinduzi Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2025 Mapinduzi Cup|2025]]

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total
|-
|align=left|[[African Cup of Nations|CAF African Cup of Nations]]
|0||1||1||2
|-
!Total !!0 !!1 !!1 !!2
|}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category|Burkina Faso national association football team}}
* [https://burkina24.com/category/actualite/sports/ L'actualité du Burkina]
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/bfa/ Burkina Faso] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/BFA Burkina Faso] at [[FIFA]]

{{Burkina Faso national football team}}
{{Navboxes|title=Finalists|list1=
{{2010 Africa Cup of Nations finalists}}
{{2013 Africa Cup of Nations finalists}}
}}
{{Navboxes|title=Squads and managers|list1=
{{Burkina Faso squad 1996 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 1998 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2000 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2002 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2004 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2010 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2012 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2013 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2015 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2017 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2021 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso squad 2023 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Burkina Faso national football team managers}}
}}
{{Football in Burkina Faso}}
{{WAFU Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Burkina Faso}}

[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:Burkina Faso national football team| ]]
[[Category:1960 establishments in Upper Volta]]
